matches.rationale: Same Edit trigger: editing files below the repository root requires reading README.md files along the directory path; the extraction is narrower to named source roots, which is partial coverage.
Same Write trigger: creating files in a subdirectory requires reading the README.md chain; the extraction is narrower to named source roots, which is partial coverage.
Same Edit target: start the filtered Next.js dev watch before editing Next.js source/tests unless already running; it omits the explicit explanation required when skipping.
Same Bash target: git checkout/switch must be followed by the full bootstrap build.
Same Write target: new test files/suites must be generated with pnpm new-test rather than manually written.
Same Bash target: save one test run's output and analyze it rather than rerunning with different grep filters.
Same Edit target: replace setTimeout-based waiting in tests with next-test-utils retry().
Same Edit target: deprecated check() is forbidden in favor of retry() plus expect().
Same Edit target: prefer fixture directories over inline files objects in test setup.
Same Bash target: a gh-created PR description must end with the NEXT_JS_LLM_PR marker; update operations are omitted.
Same Bash target for the commit portion: do not emit secret values into commits; the broader gold clauses about logs/other output are omitted.
Same Bash target: never commit local secret files; the placeholder-only documentation clause is omitted.

Despite its name, the pattern and Japanese evidence target git remote set-url after SSH failure and prohibit switching to HTTPS or mutating the URL without a request; retry/workaround clauses are omitted.
Same Bash target: prefer a lightweight SSH preflight before force-push or stack rebase that can hydrate partial-clone objects.
Same Bash target: git commits must not add Generated with Claude Code or co-author footers.
Same Bash target: pre-validate changed files with the specified formatter and linter before committing.
Same Bash target: block gh pr ready and leave the PR in draft for the user.
Same Bash target: rebuild before mode-specific Next.js integration tests when source files changed.
Same Edit target: adding a flag requires synchronized type and schema changes; the conditional define-env.ts clause is omitted, which is allowed partial coverage.
Same Edit target: force Node-import-gating flags false in define-env.ts for edge builds.
Same Edit target: react-server-dom-webpack imports must remain in entry-base.ts; consuming exports elsewhere is omitted.
Same Edit target: avoid new super:: imports and prefer crate::; the inline-mod exception is omitted.
Same Edit target: when adding highlight attributes, count actual code-block lines; the relevance and recheck clauses are omitted.
Same Edit target: newly reading a nonstandard request header must be flagged for security review.

misses.note: No Write rule starts or checks the watch process before creating a Next.js source or integration-test file; the extracted watch rule is Edit-only.
No extracted Bash rule preserves the background watch process across an iterative session.
No extracted Bash rule selects the focused test script from both mode and bundler intent.
Compiler-reported skip: "Terminating the background watch process after finishing work depends on session-level state, not a specific tool call".
Compiler-reported skip: "Choosing `pnpm --filter=next types` vs the full build depends on developer intent (type-check only), not detectable from a tool call".
No extracted Bash rule selects the filtered Next.js build for already-bootstrapped, core-only edits.
No extracted Bash rule covers the full build decision across bootstrap, CI push, and multi-package changes.
No extracted Bash rule requires --webpack when intentionally choosing Webpack for next build/dev.
Compiler-reported skip: "`--no-turbopack` flag does not exist — informational fact, not an actionable rule".
The only new-test extraction is a Write rule, so it cannot match this Bash command requirement.
No extracted Bash rule binds AI-agent pnpm new-test invocations to the documented non-interactive --args form.
Compiler-reported skip: "Running `pr-status.js` when the user asks about CI/PR status — triggered by conversational intent, not a tool call".
Compiler-reported skip: "General CI triage priority ordering (build/lint/types before tests) — a reasoning heuristic, not bound to one tool call".
Compiler-reported skip: "Treating CI failures as real until disproven and not auto-ignoring 'Known Flaky Tests' — general reasoning stance".
Compiler-reported skip: "Reproducing CI failures with the same mode/env vars — general judgment, superseded by the more specific local-repro guidance".
Compiler-reported skip: "Using mode-specific test commands when fixing module resolution/build graph issues — general debugging judgment, no single trigger".
No extracted Bash rule invokes pr-status-triage for automated analysis; the document delegates the full workflow to the referenced skill.
Compiler-reported skip: "Prioritizing CI failures over review comments, and blocking jobs first — general triage priorities, not action-bound".
Compiler-reported skip: "Mapping specific CI job failures (rust check, lint/build, test) to fix commands — reactive to CI output content, not an input-side trigger".
Compiler-reported skip: "Mapping specific CI job failures (rust check, lint/build, test) to fix commands — reactive to CI output content, not an input-side trigger".
Compiler-reported skip: "Mapping specific CI job failures (rust check, lint/build, test) to fix commands — reactive to CI output content, not an input-side trigger".
No extracted Bash rule selects development versus production test scripts according to the failing mode.
Compiler-reported skip: "Use `DEBUG=next:*` for debug logging — general tip, not an obligation tied to an action".
Compiler-reported skip: "Use `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` for local testing — general tip, not an enforced obligation".
Compiler-reported skip: "Explanation of `NODE_ENV` vs `__NEXT_DEV_SERVER` semantics — informational, no bound action".
Compiler-reported skip: "Explanation of `NODE_ENV` vs `__NEXT_DEV_SERVER` semantics — informational, no bound action".
No extracted Bash rule treats general environment-variable values as sensitive when commands access or forward them.
No extracted Bash rule combines exact CI environment-name/mode reproduction with the prohibition on literal secrets.
Compiler-reported skip: "Stop and ask the user instead of fabricating placeholder credentials when a secret is missing locally — behavioral halt, not a tool-call trigger".
Compiler-reported skip: "Keep commit messages concise/clear and focus PR descriptions on what/why — writing tone/style, not action-bindable".
Compiler-reported skip: "Keep commit messages concise/clear and focus PR descriptions on what/why — writing tone/style, not action-bindable".
No extracted Edit rule targets relative internal requires specifically in app-page.ts; the general conditional-require DCE rule is a different action.
No extracted Edit rule blocks browser.back() for router-act pages with visible accordion links; the detailed pattern is delegated to $router-act.

unmatched_extracted.note: The document gives a real Bash workflow rule to save build output once before analysis, but frozen gold has no corresponding rule.
The document gives a real Bash rule to cache gh/curl results rather than refetching the same API data, but frozen gold omits it.
The document separately forbids these footers in PR creation; gold g44 is limited by its Bash trigger_intent to Git commits.
The document contains a real Edit rule requiring require() inside compile-time if/else branches for DCE; no gold rule lists that action.
